qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: StaticDir include_dot_files(false) does not block hidden-directory file access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets `StaticDir` hidden-directory filtering in `crates/serve-static/src/dir.rs`, not the advisory's unbounded request-body reads in `Request::form_data()` / `FormData::read()` or the `Extractible` body-source path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deferred-length TUS uploads bypass the configured max_size limit
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets deferred-length upload handling in `crates/tus/src/handlers/{post,patch}.rs` and `crates/tus/src/stores/disk.rs`, not the advisory's form-data parser path that buffers request bodies in memory without a size check.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is about unbounded in-memory body collection in `Request::form_data()`, `FormData::read()`, and `Extractible` body extraction, while the session's qualifying findings are a `StaticDir` hidden-directory disclosure and a separate `salvo-tus` deferred-length upload size-limit bypass.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes denial of service from unbounded memory allocation in form-data parsing, specifically `Request::form_data()`, `FormData::read()`, multipart handling, and `#[derive(Extractible)]` body-source extraction.
`.judge/input_manifest.json` lists exactly two final `qualifying_vuln` findings in this session: `staticdir_hidden_directory_bypass` and `tus_deferred_length_max_size_bypass`.

`staticdir_hidden_directory_bypass/vulnerability_report.json` ties its root cause to `crates/serve-static/src/dir.rs` only checking the final path component before serving hidden-directory files such as `/.git/config`.

`tus_deferred_length_max_size_bypass/vulnerability_report.json` ties its root cause to `salvo-tus` deferred-length upload handling in `crates/tus/src/handlers/post.rs`, `crates/tus/src/handlers/patch.rs`, and `crates/tus/src/stores/disk.rs`, with disk growth past the configured `Tus-Max-Size`.
